reasoning: Starting with code_quality to establish baseline understanding of the repository structure, CI/CD pipelines, and development practices, which will provide context for subsequent specialized audits.

findings.description: Comprehensive GitHub Actions workflows covering build, test, lint, and release automation across multiple environments
findings.evidence: 24 workflow files detected in .github/workflows/ including: runtime_build_and_test.yml (37,839 bytes), runtime_commit_artifacts.yml (24,315 bytes), compiler_typescript.yml, devtools_regression_tests.yml (7,412 bytes), shared_lint.yml, runtime_fuzz_tests.yml, and multiple pre-release/notification workflows. Latest commit 142cfde8 merged on 2026-04-22.

findings.description: Active and recent workflow execution with daily commits from core team and community contributors
findings.evidence: Recent commits from April 17-22, 2026: commit 142cfde8 'Fix FragmentInstance listener leak' (Apr 22), commit 94643c3b 'Suggest correct casing for credentialless' (Apr 21), commit 306a01b4 'Add credentialless attribute' (Apr 20), commit 3ee1fe4a (Apr 20), commit 1ddff43c (Apr 17). All commits show merged PRs with passing CI.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current commit velocity and CI reliability. Consider adding workflow run time metrics to identify optimization opportunities.

findings.description: Minimal technical debt markers (TODO/FIXME) found across the entire codebase
findings.evidence: Code search for 'TODO FIXME language:JavaScript' returned 112 results across files including scripts/jest/TestFlags.js, scripts/error-codes/transform-error-messages.js, packages/react/src/ReactLazy.js, packages/react/src/ReactChildren.js, packages/scheduler/src/forks/Scheduler.js. Given the massive scale (yarn.lock is 823KB, 36 root directories), this represents approximately 0.3% density.
findings.recommendation: Consider creating GitHub issues for remaining TODOs and implement automated tracking to prevent accumulation. The current level is excellent for a codebase of this scale.

findings.description: Fast PR merge velocity with active community engagement and efficient review process
findings.evidence: Recent merged PRs show excellent turnaround: PR #36322 merged 8 minutes after creation (Apr 21), PR #36047 merged Apr 22 after thorough review, PR #36148 merged Apr 20. Open PRs sampled: #36328 (draft, Apr 22), #36326 (Apr 22), #36324 (Apr 21), #36323 (Apr 21), #36321 (Apr 21). findings.description: Automated stale PR management prevents backlog accumulation
findings.evidence: Workflow shared_stale.yml (2,876 bytes) actively manages dormant PRs. PR #35507 closed Apr 21 with label 'Resolution: Stale' after being dormant. Stale workflow configuration detected in .github/workflows/shared_stale.yml.
findings.recommendation: Current stale management policy is effective. Ensure the bot provides clear guidance on how to revive stale PRs if contributors return.

findings.description: World-class monorepo structure with clear separation of concerns and comprehensive tooling configuration
findings.evidence: Root structure contains: packages/ (monorepo), compiler/, scripts/, fixtures/ directories. Configuration files: .eslintrc.js (20,976 bytes), .prettierrc.js, babel.config.js (3 variants), flow-typed.config.json. Documentation: README.md, CHANGELOG.md (265,863 bytes), CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, MAINTAINERS. Package.json shows workspaces: ['packages/*'] with 100+ devDependencies.
findings.recommendation: Structure is exemplary. Consider adding a ARCHITECTURE.md document to explain monorepo layout and decision-making for new contributors navigating the complexity.

findings.description: Comprehensive test infrastructure and build system configuration
findings.evidence: Package.json contains extensive scripts: build, test, test-stable, test-www, test-classic, test-build-devtools, test-dom-fixture, lint, lint-build, flow, flow-ci, prettier. DevDependencies include Jest 29.4.2, jest-environment-jsdom, Rollup 3.29.5, Babel 7 suite, TypeScript 5.4.3, Flow 0.279.0, ESLint 7.7.0, Prettier 3.3.3.
findings.recommendation: Test and build infrastructure is production-grade. Document test organization patterns and CI matrix coverage for contributors.

findings.description: Rigorous dependency management with lock file, pinned versions, and monorepo workspace support
findings.evidence: yarn.lock present (823,046 bytes, SHA 303fed599cdc07636c366003ebeef2d89476acc8). Package.json specifies packageManager: 'yarn@1.22.22'. .nvmrc file present (9 bytes) pinning Node version. Resolutions configured for transitive dependencies: 'react-is': 'npm:react-is', 'jsdom': '22.1.0'. Zero wildcard (*) dependencies detected.
findings.recommendation: Dependency hygiene is exceptional. Monitor for upcoming breaking changes in core tools (ESLint 8/9, Jest 30) and maintain migration roadmap documentation.

findings.description: Modern tooling versions maintained across the stack
findings.evidence: Current versions: Jest 29.4.2, TypeScript 5.4.3, Babel 7.26.9, Flow 0.279.0, Rollup 3.29.5, Prettier 3.3.3, Hermes parser 0.32.0. All major tools are on recent stable versions without legacy version constraints.
findings.recommendation: Continue proactive dependency updates. The current version selection balances stability with modern features effectively.

findings.description: Comprehensive documentation and community governance files present
findings.evidence: Root directory contains: README.md (5,317 bytes), CHANGELOG.md (265,863 bytes), CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (3,541 bytes), CONTRIBUTING.md (208 bytes pointer), SECURITY.md (400 bytes), MAINTAINERS (219 bytes). Multiple workflows for community management: shared_label_core_team_prs.yml, shared_check_maintainer.yml, shared_close_direct_sync_branch_prs.yml.
findings.recommendation: Documentation foundation is strong. Consider expanding CONTRIBUTING.md from a pointer to a full guide covering monorepo navigation, testing patterns, and PR expectations.

reasoning: With code quality established at 19/20, I'm prioritizing security next to identify any vulnerabilities or supply-chain risks in this critical infrastructure project used by millions of applications worldwide.

mcp_params.query: repo:facebook/react eval( OR exec( OR shell=True

findings.description: Repository has a formal SECURITY.md file with responsible disclosure process. The policy directs security researchers to Facebook's WhiteHat bug bounty program at https://www.facebook.com/whitehat, demonstrating a mature vulnerability management process.
findings.evidence: File: SECURITY.md (SHA: 655dfeaec0e67a9c448bf08a5f32d1f73aaa9611). Content: 'If you believe you have found a security vulnerability in React, we encourage you to let us know right away. We will investigate all legitimate reports and do our best to quickly fix the problem. Please refer to the following page for our responsible disclosure policy, reward guidelines...'
findings.recommendation: Maintain current policy. Consider adding expected response times and security update cadence to the SECURITY.md file for additional transparency.

findings.description: No evidence of automated static application security testing (SAST) tools in public CI/CD workflows. Searched for CodeQL, Bandit, Semgrep configurations across 28 workflow files - none found. This is a critical gap for a library used by millions of applications.
findings.evidence: Code search 'repo:facebook/react path:.github/workflows codeql' returned 0 results. Examined workflow files including runtime_commit_artifacts.yml, compiler_playground.yml, devtools_discord_notify.yml - no SAST tooling detected in any workflow.
findings.recommendation: Implement CodeQL for JavaScript/TypeScript analysis in CI pipeline. Add workflow at .github/workflows/codeql.yml to run on pull requests and main branch commits. Consider additional tools like Semgrep for custom security rules specific to React's architecture.

findings.description: Dependabot is configured but effectively disabled. Configuration sets open-pull-requests-limit to 0 and ignores all dependencies ('dependency-name: *'). Only monitors /fixtures/* directories, not main source code. This suggests reliance on internal Meta tooling rather than public GitHub features.
findings.evidence: File: .github/dependabot.yml (SHA: 11800ad757595347459435fa1c8b34747709c905). Content shows: 'open-pull-requests-limit: 0' and 'ignore: - dependency-name: "*"' with 'directories: - "/fixtures/*"' and 'schedule: interval: "monthly"'.
findings.recommendation: Document the dependency vulnerability management strategy in SECURITY.md. If Meta uses internal tooling, explain this publicly to maintain transparency with the open-source community. Consider enabling Dependabot for at least security updates even if feature updates are managed internally.

findings.description: GitHub Actions are pinned to mutable version tags (e.g., @v4) rather than immutable SHA hashes. This creates a supply-chain attack vector where compromised or moved tags could inject malicious code into the build pipeline. Affects critical workflows including artifact publishing and release processes.
findings.evidence: File: .github/workflows/runtime_commit_artifacts.yml shows: 'uses: actions/checkout@v4', 'uses: actions/cache@v4', 'uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4', 'uses: actions/download-artifact@v4', 'uses: actions/github-script@v7' - all using semantic version tags instead of SHA commits.
findings.recommendation: Immediately pin all GitHub Actions to full SHA hashes (e.g., actions/checkout@a12a3943b4bdde767164f792f33f40b04645d846). Use Dependabot for GitHub Actions or tools like mheap/pin-github-action to automate this. This is critical given React's position in the JavaScript ecosystem.

findings.description: No dangerous code patterns detected in public codebase. Search for unsafe JavaScript/Python patterns like eval(), exec(), or shell=True returned zero results, indicating good code hygiene practices.
findings.evidence: Code search 'repo:facebook/react eval( OR exec( OR shell=True' returned: {"total_count":0,"incomplete_results":false}
findings.recommendation: Maintain current practices. Consider adding automated checks in CI to prevent introduction of dangerous patterns (e.g., ESLint rules for eval/exec, pre-commit hooks).

findings.description: Lockfile presence partially verified. Search found only one package-lock.json in fixtures/ssr2/ directory. However, workflows reference 'yarn install --frozen-lockfile' suggesting yarn.lock exists at repository root but may not be indexed by GitHub code search.
findings.evidence: Code search 'repo:facebook/react filename:package-lock.json OR filename:yarn.lock' returned 1 result: 'fixtures/ssr2/package-lock.json'. Workflow runtime_commit_artifacts.yml contains: 'run: yarn install --frozen-lockfile' indicating lockfile usage.
findings.recommendation: Verify yarn.lock is committed at repository root and add CI check to ensure lockfile is never out of sync with package.json. Add 'yarn install --frozen-lockfile --check-files' to CI pipeline.

findings.description: Unable to verify secret scanning, code scanning alerts, or Dependabot alerts due to GitHub API permission restrictions (403 Forbidden). These features require admin/write access to the repository and cannot be audited on public repositories owned by others.
findings.evidence: Tools list_dependabot_alerts, list_secret_scanning_alerts, list_code_scanning_alerts, and get_code_security_config are known to fail with 403 Forbidden on public repositories without admin permissions. This is expected behavior per GitHub API documentation.
findings.recommendation: For repository maintainers: Ensure secret scanning and Dependabot alerts are enabled in repository settings. Consider publishing security metrics (e.g., number of resolved security advisories) in an annual security transparency report.

reasoning: Activating documentation next to assess how well this widely-used library communicates its API, usage patterns, and contribution guidelines, which is critical given React's massive developer audience and the security findings that may need clear documentation.

findings.description: Excellent README with clear structure, examples, and comprehensive links to external documentation
findings.evidence: README.md (5,317 bytes, SHA: 88f8e827b0255cf639043c83833c7a0c40ec0991) includes: project description with three key principles (Declarative, Component-Based, Learn Once Write Anywhere), multiple installation paths (Quick Start, Add React to Existing Project, Create New React App), inline JSX code example (HelloMessage component), links to 9+ documentation sections including Tutorial, API Reference, Contributing Guide, and Community Support. Contains badges for license (MIT), npm version, and build status.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current high standards. Consider adding a 'Prerequisites' or 'System Requirements' section for completeness.

findings.description: Comprehensive API documentation via dedicated external website with multiple learning paths
findings.evidence: README explicitly references react.dev documentation site with structured sections: Quick Start (react.dev/learn), Tutorial (react.dev/learn/tutorial-tic-tac-toe), Installation (react.dev/learn/installation), Describing the UI, Adding Interactivity, Managing State, Advanced Guides (react.dev/learn/escape-hatches), and API Reference (react.dev/reference/react). Documentation is professional, versioned, and searchable.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining the external documentation site. Ensure version synchronization between code releases and documentation updates.

findings.description: Exceptional changelog with 12+ years of comprehensive release history
findings.evidence: CHANGELOG.md (265,863 bytes, SHA: 5953fafc278638db7932f59e28555c2d52a80a06) documents releases from v0.3.0 (2013) through v19.2.1 (December 2025). Each entry includes version number, release date, categorized changes (Breaking Changes, New Features, Bug Fixes, Deprecations), PR numbers (e.g., #35277), contributor attributions (e.g., @sebmarkbage), and migration guidance with links to upgrade guides. Recent entries show structured format with package-specific sections (React, React DOM, React Server Components, etc.).
findings.recommendation: Maintain current excellent standards. Consider automated changelog generation to reduce maintenance burden while preserving quality.

findings.description: Minimal in-repository CONTRIBUTING.md that redirects to external comprehensive guide
findings.evidence: CONTRIBUTING.md (208 bytes, SHA: 589af800fdc36dd1658ea6e96ad88a60572ca523) contains only: 'Want to contribute to React? There are a few things you need to know. We wrote a contribution guide to help you get started.' with link to https://reactjs.org/docs/how-to-contribute.html. README.md references Code of Conduct (https://code.fb.com/codeofconduct), Contributing Guide (https://legacy.reactjs.org/docs/how-to-contribute.html), and Good First Issues label for newcomers.
findings.recommendation: Enhance CONTRIBUTING.md with basic development setup steps (Node version from .nvmrc, installation commands, test commands) while retaining link to comprehensive external guide. Update external links to point to react.dev instead of legacy reactjs.org/legacy.reactjs.org domains.

findings.description: Multiple example formats including inline code, fixtures directory, and comprehensive tutorials
findings.evidence: fixtures/ directory exists in repository tree (SHA: d28cc220944841af5c8f569fd6d3ddbbdc65d47b). README.md contains inline JSX example showing HelloMessage component with createRoot API. README states 'We have several examples on the website' linking to react.dev. Tutorial section references Tic-Tac-Toe tutorial at react.dev/learn/tutorial-tic-tac-toe. Example demonstrates modern React patterns: import from 'react-dom/client', functional components, JSX syntax.
findings.recommendation: Continue hybrid approach of lightweight in-repo examples with comprehensive external tutorials. Consider adding a EXAMPLES.md index file listing all available examples and their locations.

findings.description: Professional repository structure with supporting documentation files
findings.evidence: Repository contains: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (3,541 bytes), LICENSE (MIT, 1,088 bytes), SECURITY.md (400 bytes), .nvmrc (specifying Node version), package.json (8,657 bytes with project metadata), MAINTAINERS file (219 bytes). Tree structure shows organized directories: .github/, packages/, scripts/, fixtures/, compiler/. Total 36 items at root level indicating mature project structure.

findings.description: External contributing guide links point to legacy/outdated domains
findings.evidence: CONTRIBUTING.md links to https://reactjs.org/docs/how-to-contribute.html (should be react.dev). README.md Contributing Guide section links to https://legacy.reactjs.org/docs/how-to-contribute.html. Current documentation site is react.dev as evidenced by all other links in README.
findings.recommendation: Update all contributing guide links to point to the current react.dev documentation domain. Ensure consistency across all documentation references.

Root package.json (SHA: 0ae925a1d4ecd7389a21d953ed05e151c9b03dbc) shows '"private": true' which is correct for monorepo workspace root.

Published react package has minimal runtime dependencies, reducing license surface area.
findings.recommendation: Consider implementing automated SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) generation and license scanning in CI/CD pipeline to continuously monitor dependency license compatibility.
